{"analysis":"Now I can see clearly! Two copies of the zip data exist in the dat file, each with different halves corrupted:

Copy 1 (offset 1048576): First 20 bytes readable as `PASSWORD=8XDP5Q2RT9Z`, last 13 bytes corrupted binary
Copy 2 (offset 3145632): First 20 bytes corrupted binary, last 13 bytes readable as `K7VB3BV4WW54\n`

Combining both halves: `PASSWORD=8XDP5Q2RT9ZK7VB3BV4WW54\n`

Password = `8XDP5Q2RT9ZK7VB3BV4WW54` (23 chars, starts with 8XD, ends with W54, uppercase+digits only)","plan":"Write the recovered password to /app/recovered_passwords.txt and verify it meets all criteria.","commands":[{"keystrokes":"echo '8XDP5Q2RT9ZK7VB3BV4WW54' > /app/recovered_passwords.txt
","duration":0.1},{"keystrokes":"cat /app/recovered_passwords.txt
","duration":0.1},{"keystrokes":"echo -n '8XDP5Q2RT9ZK7VB3BV4WW54' | wc -c
","duration":0.1}]}
